prompt: Create a Kubernetes ConfigMap named app-config in the default namespace with two key-value pairs: APP_ENV=production and LOG_LEVEL=info.
common_failures: Wrong apiVersion (v1 is correct for ConfigMap)

prompt: Create a Kubernetes Opaque Secret named db-credentials in the backend namespace containing base64-encoded fields: username (dXNlcg==) and password (cGFzc3dvcmQ=).
Create a Kubernetes Namespace named data-pipeline with labels team=data-engineering and cost-center=analytics.
Create a ServiceAccount named ci-deployer in the ci-cd namespace with the annotation iam.amazonaws.com/role set to arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/deployer.
Create a ResourceQuota named team-quota in the team-alpha namespace limiting: requests.cpu=4, requests.memory=8Gi, limits.cpu=8, limits.memory=16Gi, and pods=20.
Create a LimitRange named default-limits in the sandbox namespace that sets default container CPU request to 100m, CPU limit to 500m, memory request to 128Mi, and memory limit to 256Mi.
Create a PersistentVolumeClaim named data-pvc in the stateful-apps namespace requesting 10Gi of ReadWriteOnce storage using the fast-ssd StorageClass.
Create a minimal Kubernetes Pod named debug-pod in the ops namespace running busybox:1.36 with command sleep 3600. Set CPU request to 50m and memory request to 64Mi.
Create a ClusterIP Service named api-service in the backend namespace that exposes port 8080 targeting containerPort 8080 on pods with label app=api.
Create a Kubernetes Deployment named nginx-static in the web namespace running nginx:1.27 with 2 replicas. Set CPU request 100m/limit 250m and memory request 128Mi/limit 256Mi.

Create a Kubernetes Deployment named user-api with 3 replicas running myregistry/user-api:v1.5 and a ClusterIP Service named user-api-svc exposing port 3000 in the backend namespace. Ensure the Service selector matches the Deployment pod labels.
common_failures: Service selector does not match Deployment pod template labels
Port mismatch between Service and container

prompt: Create a headless StatefulSet named postgres with 3 replicas running postgres:15 and a headless Service named postgres in the databases namespace. The StatefulSet should use a volumeClaimTemplate requesting 20Gi ReadWriteOnce from storageclass standard.
common_failures: Missing clusterIP: None for headless service

prompt: Create a ConfigMap named app-settings with key APP_ENV=production and DB_HOST=postgres.databases.svc.cluster.local, and a Deployment named settings-consumer that mounts these as environment variables using configMapKeyRef.
Create a Kubernetes Secret named db-creds with fields username and password, and a Deployment named order-service that injects these as environment variables DB_USER and DB_PASS using secretKeyRef.
common_failures: Hardcoding secrets in env value instead of secretKeyRef

prompt: Create a Deployment named web-frontend with 2 replicas running nginx:1.27 and an HorizontalPodAutoscaler targeting web-frontend that scales between 2 and 10 replicas at 70% CPU utilization. Use autoscaling/v2 API.
Create a Kubernetes Job named data-migration in the ops namespace that runs the image myregistry/migrator:v3.0 once to completion. Set backoffLimit to 3 and activeDeadlineSeconds to 600.

Create a CronJob named db-backup in the ops namespace running myregistry/backup:v2.1 every day at 2:00 AM UTC. Set restartPolicy to OnFailure, successfulJobsHistoryLimit to 3, and failedJobsHistoryLimit to 1.
Create a DaemonSet named node-exporter in the monitoring namespace running prom/node-exporter:v1.7.0. The DaemonSet should mount /proc and /sys from the host and set hostPID: true.
Create a ServiceAccount named api-sa in the backend namespace, and a Deployment named api-server that uses this ServiceAccount. The Deployment should run myregistry/api:v2.0 with 2 replicas.
common_failures: ServiceAccount in different namespace than Deployment

prompt: Create a PersistentVolumeClaim named app-data (10Gi, ReadWriteOnce, standard StorageClass) and a Deployment named data-processor that mounts this PVC at /data inside the container.
common_failures: Volume name mismatch between volumes and volumeMounts

prompt: Create a StatefulSet named elasticsearch with 3 replicas running elasticsearch:8.11, an HPA scaling from 3 to 9 replicas at 60% CPU, and a PVC template requesting 50Gi of fast-ssd storage. Apply appropriate securityContext and RBAC ServiceAccount controls.

Create a Deployment named payment-service with 3 replicas, an HPA scaling 3-10 at 70% CPU, and a PodDisruptionBudget ensuring minAvailable=2 at all times. Apply appropriate securityContext and RBAC ServiceAccount controls.
common_failures: PDB selector not matching Deployment labels

prompt: Create a multi-container Pod named log-processor in namespace logging with a main container running myregistry/processor:v1.2 and a sidecar container running fluent/fluent-bit:2.2 that shares an emptyDir volume at /var/log. Apply appropriate securityContext and RBAC ServiceAccount controls.

Create a Deployment named app-server with an init container that runs myregistry/db-migrator:v1.0 to completion before the main container myregistry/app:v2.3 starts. The init container should exit 0 on success. Apply appropriate securityContext and RBAC ServiceAccount controls.
common_failures: initContainers placed at wrong level in spec
Main container does not wait for init container

prompt: Create a Deployment named web-app in namespace production and a NetworkPolicy named web-app-netpol that allows ingress on port 8080 only from pods with label role=frontend, and egress only to pods with label role=database on port 5432. Apply appropriate securityContext and RBAC ServiceAccount controls.

Create a headless StatefulSet named zookeeper with 3 replicas running zookeeper:3.9 and a headless Service named zookeeper in namespace messaging. Include readinessProbe on port 2181 and livenessProbe. Apply appropriate securityContext and RBAC ServiceAccount controls.
common_failures: serviceName not pointing to headless service

prompt: Create a Deployment named blog-api, a ClusterIP Service named blog-api-svc on port 8080, and an Ingress named blog-ingress routing api.example.com/v1 to blog-api-svc using the nginx ingress class with TLS termination. Apply appropriate securityContext and RBAC ServiceAccount controls.

Create a CronJob named report-generator in namespace analytics that runs myregistry/reporter:v1.5 daily at 06:00 UTC and mounts a PVC named report-storage at /reports. Apply appropriate securityContext and RBAC ServiceAccount controls.
common_failures: PVC access mode incompatible with CronJob (use ReadWriteMany or ReadWriteOncePod)

prompt: Create a ConfigMap named svc-config, a Secret named svc-secret, a Deployment named microservice that mounts the ConfigMap as env vars and the Secret as a volume at /etc/secrets, and a ClusterIP Service exposing port 9000. Apply appropriate securityContext and RBAC ServiceAccount controls.

Create a Deployment named multi-port-app exposing ports 8080 (HTTP) and 9090 (metrics), and a Service that exposes both ports with named ports http and metrics. Apply appropriate securityContext and RBAC ServiceAccount controls.
common_failures: Port names not matching between Service and containerPorts

prompt: Create a Deployment named secure-api in namespace production running myregistry/api:v3.0 with full Pod Security Standards restricted securityContext: runAsNonRoot=true, runAsUser=1000, allowPrivilegeEscalation=false, readOnlyRootFilesystem=true, drop ALL capabilities, and seccompProfile RuntimeDefault.

Create a NetworkPolicy named strict-netpol in namespace production that: allows ingress to port 8080 only from namespace monitoring (label env=monitoring) and namespace frontend, blocks all other ingress, allows egress only to port 5432 in namespace databases and port 53 UDP for DNS.
common_failures: namespaceSelector not combined with podSelector correctly

prompt: Create RBAC resources for a deployment pipeline: a ServiceAccount named deploy-sa, a Role named deploy-role that allows get/list/watch on pods and create/update on deployments, and a RoleBinding named deploy-rb binding the role to the ServiceAccount. All in namespace ci-cd.
common_failures: Using wildcard (*) in verbs or resources

prompt: Create a Pod named hardened-job in namespace security-test running myregistry/scanner:v1.0 with seccompProfile type RuntimeDefault at the pod level and container level, and drop ALL Linux capabilities. Set automountServiceAccountToken: false.
common_failures: seccompProfile at wrong YAML nesting level

prompt: Create a Deployment named restricted-app with readOnlyRootFilesystem=true, runAsNonRoot=true, allowPrivilegeEscalation=false, and drop ALL capabilities. Add an emptyDir volume at /tmp for writable scratch space. Run as user 65534 (nobody).
common_failures: runAsUser at pod level instead of container level

prompt: Create a PodDisruptionBudget named critical-pdb ensuring maxUnavailable=1 for a Deployment named critical-service with 5 replicas in namespace production. Include appropriate pod anti-affinity rules to spread across nodes.
common_failures: PDB selector not matching Deployment pod labels

prompt: Create a NetworkPolicy named isolate-namespace in namespace tenant-a that denies all ingress and egress by default, then adds exceptions to allow ingress from same namespace only and egress to kube-dns (port 53 UDP/TCP) only.
common_failures: Missing DNS egress exception causes pod DNS failures

prompt: Create a Deployment named db-client running myregistry/client:v1.0 with runAsUser=1001, fsGroup=2000, supplementalGroups=[3000], and readOnlyRootFilesystem=true. Mount a secret as a volume with mode 0400.
common_failures: fsGroup at container level instead of pod securityContext

prompt: Create a ResourceQuota named strict-quota and LimitRange named strict-limits in namespace regulated enforcing: max 2 CPUs total, 4Gi memory, 5 pods, default container CPU limit 200m, default memory limit 128Mi, and max container CPU 500m.
common_failures: ResourceQuota using wrong resource name format

prompt: Create a Deployment named hpa-secure-app with HPA (3-12 replicas, 65% CPU) and PDB (minAvailable=2). The Deployment must have runAsNonRoot=true, allowPrivilegeEscalation=false, drop ALL caps, and readOnlyRootFilesystem=true.

Create a 3-tier application: frontend Deployment (nginx:1.27, 3 replicas) + LoadBalancer Service on port 80, backend Deployment (myregistry/api:v2.0, 3 replicas) + ClusterIP Service on port 8080, and a Redis StatefulSet (redis:7.2, 3 replicas) + headless Service. All in namespace three-tier with appropriate securityContexts, resource limits, and health probes.
common_failures: Missing health probes on all tiers
Redis headless service missing clusterIP: None

prompt: Create a microservices setup in namespace microservices: ServiceAccount, Role, RoleBinding (RBAC for pod reader), a NetworkPolicy allowing inter-service traffic only on port 8080, an Ingress with TLS routing to a frontend Service, and a Deployment with full PSS restricted securityContext.

Create a multi-namespace RBAC setup: Namespace team-a, Namespace team-b, a ClusterRole named read-pods allowing get/list/watch on pods cluster-wide, and RoleBindings in each namespace binding team-a-sa and team-b-sa ServiceAccounts to the ClusterRole. Ensure automountServiceAccountToken: false on all ServiceAccounts.
common_failures: Using ClusterRoleBinding instead of RoleBinding for namespace-scoped access

prompt: Create a fully PSS-restricted application: Deployment named secure-web (runAsNonRoot, runAsUser=1000, allowPrivilegeEscalation=false, readOnlyRootFilesystem=true, drop ALL, seccompProfile RuntimeDefault), ClusterIP Service, and Ingress with TLS using cert-manager annotation cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer=letsencrypt. Namespace: secure-apps.
common_failures: TLS secret not referenced in Ingress

prompt: Create a StatefulSet named mysql (mysql:8.0, 1 replica) with a 50Gi PVC template, a CronJob named mysql-backup running every 6 hours using myregistry/mysql-backup:v1.0, a ServiceAccount with RBAC Role/RoleBinding allowing the CronJob to list pods, all in namespace databases.
common_failures: CronJob RBAC not scoped to correct namespace
StatefulSet serviceName not matching Service name

prompt: Create a blue-green deployment pair: Deployment named app-blue (myregistry/app:v1.0, 5 replicas) and Deployment named app-green (myregistry/app:v2.0, 0 replicas) with a Service named app-svc whose selector can switch between them using a slot label. Include a PodDisruptionBudget for the active deployment.
common_failures: Service selector not using slot label

prompt: Create a canary deployment setup: a stable Deployment named api-stable (myregistry/api:v1.0, 9 replicas) and a canary Deployment named api-canary (myregistry/api:v2.0, 1 replica) sharing a common Service selector label app=api. Add an HPA on api-stable (5-20 replicas, 70% CPU).
common_failures: Canary and stable not sharing common Service selector
HPA targeting canary instead of stable

prompt: Create a complete production app in namespace production-app: Deployment named app (3 replicas, full securityContext), NetworkPolicy allowing only frontend ingress and database egress, HPA (3-15, 70% CPU), PDB (minAvailable=2), ClusterIP Service, and RBAC (ServiceAccount + Role + RoleBinding for config map reading).
common_failures: PDB selector not aligned with HPA target

prompt: Create a namespace named regulated with: ResourceQuota (10 CPUs, 20Gi, 30 pods), LimitRange (default CPU 200m, memory 256Mi, max CPU 2, max memory 4Gi), NetworkPolicy denying all traffic by default, and a Namespace-scoped Role allowing only configmap get/list.
common_failures: LimitRange type not set to Container

prompt: Create a complete 3-tier app in namespace fullstack: frontend Deployment+LoadBalancer Service+Ingress with TLS, backend Deployment+ClusterIP Service with RBAC (ServiceAccount+Role+RoleBinding), database StatefulSet+headless Service, NetworkPolicy restricting traffic between tiers, and HPA on backend (3-10, 70% CPU). Apply full PSS restricted securityContext on all Deployments.
